subsystem_candidates: SwiftUI hosted row root/container in LogRowHostedView

supporting_evidence: Current row-local probes show stable internal geometry and seam deltas in collapse traces.
User reports the same visible top-edge jump still reproduces despite prior probe-oriented refinements.
contradicting_evidence: The issue is a rendered top-edge artifact, not a state-promotion mismatch between tentative and committed channels.
Prior instrumentation focused on internal SwiftUI geometry proxies that may not be the true visible owner.
missing_evidence: A single correlated trace that identifies which actual rendered owner layer (cell/contentView/host root/card border/clip/sibling occlusion) moves when the user-visible top edge jumps.
candidates.next_discriminating_check: Add direct owner-stack probes at collapse start for the candidate visible-owner layers and compare first movement timestamp/delta against the user-visible top-edge jump.

phase_ownership.best_supported_hypothesis: The visible jump is owned by a rendered container/layer outside currently trusted internal layout proxies.
phase_ownership.first_diverging_owner: Unknown until owner-stack correlation probes are added

retrieval.next_action_mode: Run one narrow visible-owner correlation instrumentation pass; no bug fix
